name: environmental_health_emergency_exemption_harbor_rig_utl
premise: A Environmental Health response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving environmental health emergency exemption harbor rig utl. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before markets open. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as rights vs utility under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Mira Chandra
agents.identity: I am Mira Chandra, accountable for a defensible call in Environmental Health when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays utility for rights.

agents.name: Gideon Silva
agents.identity: I am Gideon Silva, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Environmental Health.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so utility isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of rights.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect utility.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to utility to secure rights under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before markets open. Evidence is partial. Mira Chandra must decide under rights vs utility. What does Mira Chandra do?
